title: Persistence-Interface Toy: a pre-registered experiment on compact interfaces for persistence
description:
A pre-registered computational experiment. For a system whose persistence depends on a set of binary constraints, does a compact interface predict persistence nearly as well as the full microscopic model, and better than an additive baseline, at lower description length? Two senses are tested separately: QUANTITY (identity-blind power sums) and STRUCTURE (sparse, specific couplings).

The design, the locked predictions P0-P4, and the adjudication rule were committed before any model code; the alpha=0 additive null and a held-out-configuration split are integrity guards; locked predictions were not edited after the run. The experiment is fully deterministic (master seed 20260627; pinned library versions recorded). Out of scope: any claim that a compact interface is "more than statistics" or a causal addition over the micro model.
